document.author: United Nations Department of Peacekeeping Operations
document.summary: This document outlines the evolution and principles of United Nations peacekeeping operations over the past six decades. It serves as a guide for planners and practitioners, capturing lessons learned from over 60 operations since 1948. The publication emphasizes the complexity of modern peacekeeping missions, which now include a range of activities beyond monitoring cease-fires, such as promoting national dialogue, protecting civilians, and supporting the rule of law.
key_takeaways: United Nations peacekeeping has evolved significantly since 1948.
Modern missions involve complex tasks beyond simple monitoring.
The document serves as a guide for effective planning and execution of peacekeeping operations.
It reflects lessons learned from past operations to improve future missions.
The ongoing reform effort aims to professionalize peacekeeping operations.
Clear guidance is essential for personnel involved in peacekeeping.
document.why_it_matters: This document is significant as it codifies the experiences and lessons learned from decades of United Nations peacekeeping, providing essential guidance for current and future operations. It addresses the challenges of contemporary peacekeeping and aims to enhance the effectiveness of international responses to crises.

claims.claim_text: United Nations peacekeeping operations have evolved into a complex global undertaking.
claims.evidence_text: Over the past six decades, United Nations peacekeeping has evolved into a complex, global undertaking.

claims.claim_text: The number of personnel in peacekeeping operations has reached unprecedented levels.
claims.evidence_text: Since the beginning of the new millennium, the number of military, police and civilian personnel deployed in United Nations peacekeeping operations around the world has reached unprecedented levels.

claims.claim_text: The document serves as a key guidance for United Nations peacekeeping operations.
claims.evidence_text: The present publication... represents the first attempt in over a decade to codify the major lessons learned from the past six decades of United Nations peacekeeping experience.

entities.name: United Nations
entities.description: An international organization founded in 1945 to promote peace, security, and cooperation among countries.
entities.evidence_text: United Nations peacekeeping has evolved into a complex, global undertaking.

entities.name: Department of Peacekeeping Operations
entities.description: A department of the United Nations responsible for managing peacekeeping operations.
entities.evidence_text: United Nations Department of Peacekeeping Operations (DPKO) and the Department of Field Support (DFS) have embarked on a major reform effort.

events.event: Approval of the document on United Nations Peacekeeping Operations Principles and Guidelines

citations.title: None
citations.evidence_text: This document captures these experiences for the benefit and guidance of planners and practitioners of United Nations peacekeeping operations.
